data.title: Hegseth is said to want 8% shift in defense spending
data.description:
U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is aiming to cut military spending over the next five years and shift the funds to defense priorities of the Trump administration, Bloomberg News reported Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The Army, Navy, Air Force, Marines, Space Force and civilian agencies will be asked to propose 8% reductions to their spending forecasts for fiscal year 2026 and several years after, according a memo being drafted for Hegseth.

Included on the chopping block will be excessive bureaucracy and spending on programs related to climate change and diversity, equity and inclusion, Bloomberg News reported, citing unnamed sources. Such proposals would be line with President Donald Trump’s efforts.

The shares of several defense companies such as Lockheed Martin (NYSE:LMT) and Northrop Grumman (NYSE:NOC) fell sharply last week after Trump said the United States might be able to slash defense spending in half after discussions with adversaries China and Russia. Trump also has pushed European allies to increase their defense spending and has said he wants to work toward an end to the war in Ukraine, which has received billions in military and humanitarian aid from the United States.

Hegseth’s team is making revisions to the predecessor administration’s notional spending plans, which estimated expenditures of $876.8 billion for fiscal year 2026, up from $849 billion this year. The defense secretary also said the Pentagon wants to cooperate with Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency, which is seeking to cut federal spending.

A Pentagon spokesperson told Bloomberg News that he didn’t have further information about Hegseth’s spending proposals.

data.title: Investors seeking alpha should focus on 3 industries: Goldman Sachs
data.description:
Investors seeking to outperform the market should focus their stock-picking efforts on volatile names in the consumer discretionary, information technology and communications services industries, strategists at financial-services firm Goldman Sachs said.

Their recommendation is based on last year’s wide dispersion of yearly returns for stocks that make up the Standard & Poor’s 500 stock index (SP500). In addition to the wide range of returns, the stock moves were more driven by company-specific factors than macroeconomic trends compared with the historical average.

“S&P 500 annual return dispersion in 2024 rose to 70 percentage points, the highest level outside of recessions since 2007,” David Kostin, strategist at Goldman Sachs, said in a February 14 report. “High return dispersion reflects a favorable stock-picking environment.”

In the past six months, returns for 74% of S&P 500 stocks were the result of individual performance as compared with the two-decade average of 58%, according to Goldman’s research.

This “micro-driven,” company-specific environment is likely to persist in 2025 because of favorable economic conditions, the continued development and adoption of artificial intelligence technology “should create differentiation” among stocks and uncertainties with the Trump administration’s policies.

After President Trump announced tariffs aimed at Mexico, Canada and China, the Economic Policy Uncertainty Index – a metric based on the number of newspaper articles about policy uncertainty, changes to the tax code and the Federal Reserve Bank of Philadelphia's Survey of Professional Forecasters, among other factors – at the end of January jumped to the highest level since the beginning of the Covid-19 pandemic.

“Typically, higher levels of economic policy uncertainty coincide with higher levels of return dispersion as investors discern the winners and losers of proposed economic policy,” according to Goldman.

25 stocks with ‘micro-driven’ volatility

Goldman’s analysts calculated a “dispersion score” for each S&P 500 stock to help identify the best possible picks. This score is based on how much of each stock’s movements can be explained by company-specific factors and a forecast of volatility associated with those factors.

“Stocks with high dispersion scores are as likely to generate large alpha through underperformance as through outperformance,” according to Goldman. “In contrast, more macro-driven sectors such as Real Estate and Utilities have the lowest dispersion scores, indicating less alpha-generating opportunity.”

The bank created a list of 25 stocks based on its dispersion score.

data.title: TSMC, Broadcom consider possible Intel deals to split chipmaker: report
data.description:
Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) rivals Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE:TSM) and Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) are considering deals that could split the troubled U.S. chipmaker into two, the Wall Street Journal reported Saturday, citing people familiar with the matter.

Broadcom (AVGO) has been closely examining Intel's (INTC) chip design and marketing business, the report said, but will only make a bid if it can find a partner for Intel's (INTC) manufacturing business.

TSMC (TSM), the world's biggest contract chipmaker, has studied running all of Intel's (INTC) chip plants, either as part of an investor consortium or other structure, the report said.

The discussions with possible suitors and Trump administration officials, who are concerned about the company seen as crucial to national security, are being led by Intel's (INTC) executive chairman Frank Yeary, the report said.

According to the report, Yeary has been telling people close to him that he is focused on maximizing value for Intel (INTC) shareholders.

Intel (INTC), Broadcom (AVGO) and TSMC (TSM) did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's requests for comment.

President Donald Trump's administration may not support Intel's (INTC) chip plants being run by a foreign player, Reuters reported Friday. While the White House supports foreign companies investing in the U.S., it is "unlikely" to support a foreign entity controlling Intel's (INTC) plants.

Bloomberg had earlier reported that TSMC (TSM) was evaluating acquiring a controlling stake in Intel's (INTC) factories at Trump's request.

Any deal for Intel (INTC) would require a signoff from the U.S. government, which has poured billions of dollars into Intel (INTC) under the Chips Act of 2022. The ailing chipmaker has been the largest beneficiary of the $53 billion program, receiving up to $7.9 billion to build new factories in Ohio, Arizona and other locations in the U.S.

Intel (INTC) set up its foundry business under the leadership of former CEO Pat Gelsinger, who embarked on an ambitious turnaround plan. But Gelsinger was forced out of the company last year after the board reportedly lost confidence in him.

Intel (INTC), currently valued at $102.19 billion, has tumbled nearly 65% in the last five years. In comparison, TSMC's (TSM) current market capitalization stands at $840.25 billion and Broadcom's (AVGO) at $1.09 trillion.

More on Broadcom, Intel

data.title: Top global stories this week: Nippon Steel, BP, Nestlé and Nissan among notable names
data.description:
Globally, all major stock indexes ticked upwards on a weekly basis, as investor sentiment strengthened after U.S. President Donald Trump sparked hopes about resolving the Ukraine conflict and delayed a decision on imposing additional tariffs on imported goods.

Tariffs took center stage this week, with Trump on Monday announcing 25% levies on all steel and aluminum imports into the U.S. On Thursday, Trump signed a memorandum that tasked his administration to investigate current trade relations and propose reciprocal tariffs on a country-specific basis. While awaiting more details on the import tariffs, traders remain hopeful that negotiations will avert the levies.

Meanwhile, the latest producer inflation report from the U.S. came in hotter-than-expected, following strong consumer inflation data, earlier in the week. However, the January retail sales data weakened more than expected.

The world’s largest economy ended a two-week losing streak. Nasdaq 100 (COMP:IND) gained 1.2%, S&P 500 index SP500 was up 0.8% and Dow Jones (DJI) advanced 0.5% in the week.

Across the Atlantic, the European equities (STOXX) notched fresh record highs for four consecutive sessions, advancing 1.5% in the week. London (UKX) also registered all-time high levels of 8,807 in the week, adding ~ 0.4% weekly. France (CAC:IND) and Germany (DAX:IND) ended the week ~2.6%, and ~3.3% higher, respectively.

Major news from Europe included a significant decline in oil major profits, strong earnings from technology and luxury firms, and a cautious outlook from consumer goods companies.

BP (BP) chief executive, Murray Auchincloss vowed fundamental reset of the company's strategy after reporting a 35% drop in annual profits, falling short of analyst expectations. Activist investor Elliot Management is reported to have taken a significant stake in the energy company, intensifying demands for strategic shifts.

Technology firm Siemens (OTCPK:SIEGY) reported an increase in Q1 net profit that beat market expectations, as the company continues to benefit from datacenter demand.

Nestlé (OTCPK:NSRGY) reported modestly stronger-than-expected annual sales growth on Thursday, driven by price hikes, but the consumer giant cautioned of a tighter profit margin in 2025.

Ferrari (RACE) saw a 14% increase in Q4 sales, profits surpassed Wall Street expectations, driven by strong demand for personalized vehicles and a favorable product mix. However, the sports car company issued cautious guidance for the year, anticipating higher supply chain costs and a higher tax rate due to changes in Italy’s tax incentive program.

French luxury group Hermes (OTCPK:HESAF) reported fourth-quarter results well above analyst expectations, showing robust appetite from wealthy consumers for its most expensive items including Birkin bags.

On the economic front, the Eurozone economy grew 0.1% in Q4 2024, surpassing expectations of no growth, though it remained the weakest performance of the year. UK economy eked out 0.1% growth in the fourth quarter, beating expectations of 0.1% contraction in the economy.

Germany's annual inflation rate dropped to 2.3% in January 2025, down from 2.6% in December, in line with expectations.

In Asian markets, China (SHCOMP) grew 0.7% and Hong Kong (HSI) rose 4.2%, while Japan added nearly 1% in the last week.

Corporate news revolved around earnings, and acquisition interests.

Nissan (OTCPK:NSANF) revised its revenue guidance downward over higher variable marketing expenses and lower wholesales, following lower Q3 results.

Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) will keep its plan to purchase US Steel (X) and will notify the US government of its intentions, although President Donald Trump has voiced his opposition to the $14B purchase.

Honda Motor (HMC) reaffirmed its FY 2025 financial forecasts and expects motorcycle unit sales to reach 20.6M for FY 2025, driven by strong demand in Asia, particularly in India and Brazil.

SoftBank (OTCPK:SOBKY) reported higher nine-month revenue and announced that the company is advancing efforts in AI-RAN, leveraging NVIDIA (NVDA) technology to transform radio base stations into super decentralized AI data centers.

Apple (AAPL) is working with Alibaba (BABA) to bring artificial intelligence features to iPhones in China. The two companies have submitted the AI features to China's cyberspace regulator for approval.

XPeng (XPEV) announced its official entry to the UK market on Wednesday and initiated the pre-sale of its first right-hand drive model.

BYD Company (OTCPK:BYDDF) announced new advanced driver-assistance features across most of its lineup for no extra cost. Shares hit record high.

Meanwhile, a recent industry research showed the purchase of chipmaking equipment by China is set to decline this year after three years of growth, amid challenges of overcapacity and U.S. export curbs.

The People’s Bank of China pledged to support the economy with loose monetary policy and forecast stable economic growth for China in 2025, as stimulus measures implemented last year begin to revive production, demand, and market sentiment. Asian investors also found support as Trump delayed implementing reciprocal tariffs.

China inflation came in at 0.5% over the year, exceeding expectations of 0.4%.

ETFs to track Europe: ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

ETFs to track Asia & Oceania: (NYSEARCA:EWJ), (NYSE:JEQ), (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:EWH), (NYSEARCA:DXJ), (NYSE:CAF), (NYSEARCA:FXY), (NYSEARCA:YINN), (NYSEARCA:YANG), (NASDAQ:CXSE), (NYSEARCA:KWEB)

data.title: Earnings week ahead: BABA, XYZ, BIDU, WMT, RIVN and more
data.description:
Despite a holiday-shortened week, investors will have plenty to track, with a wide array of earnings reports spanning multiple sectors. Key reports to watch include Alibaba Group Holding (BABA), Block (XYZ) and Walmart (WMT), representing the technology and retail sectors. Additionally, Rivian Automotive (RIVN) will provide insights about the EV industry.

In the energy space, Occidental Petroleum (OXY) and Devon Energy (DVN) are expected to provide important updates, while Baidu (BIDU) and Unity Software (U) will offer results from the technology sector. The mining sector will also be in focus, with earnings reports from Newmont (NEM), Vale (VALE) and Pan American Silver (PAAS).

Medtronic (MDT), VICI Properties (VICI), Etsy (ETSY), Arista Networks (ANET), Booking Holdings (BKNG), Innovative Industrial Properties (IIPR) and Blue Owl Capital (OBDC) are also set to announce their quarterly results during the week.

Below is a rundown of major quarterly updates anticipated in the week of February 17 to February 21:

Tuesday, February 18

Baidu (BIDU)

With the U.S. stock market closed on Monday for a holiday, Chinese search giant Baidu (BIDU) provides the first major earnings set piece of the week, with results due out before the market opens on Monday. Analysts are forecasting a 38% decline in profits.

Recently, the company made waves by announcing that its AI chatbot, Ernie Bot, will be available for free to all users starting April 1. This move is seen as an effort to help Baidu regain momentum in the AI race.

CEO Robin Li emphasized that despite the cost efficiency demonstrated by DeepSeek compared to large AI models, continued investment in data centers and cloud infrastructure remains essential.

Baidu currently gets a Hold rating from Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system, while sell-side analysts suggest a Buy.

In the face of market skepticism and weaker recent growth, Seeking Alpha Investing Leader Stone Fox Capital remains optimistic, noting that Baidu has "very promising AI and robotaxi technologies."

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $1.87
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $4.57B
  • Earnings Insight: The company has topped EPS estimates in 7 of the past 8 quarters, exceeding revenue expectations in 6 of those reports.

Also reporting: Occidental Petroleum (OXY), Medtronic (MDT), Devon Energy (DVN), Arista Networks (ANET), iQIYI (IQ), Star Bulk Carriers (SBLK), Genuine Parts Company (GPC), Genworth Financial (GNW), EQT Corporation (EQT), Cadence Design Systems (CDNS), BrightSpire Capital (BRSP), Fluor Corporation (FLR), Toll Brothers (TOL), Gladstone Commercial (GOOD), Industrial Logistics Properties Trust (ILPT), Vulcan Materials Company (VMC), The Chemours (CC), Bumble (BMBL) and more.

Wednesday, February 19

Etsy (ETSY)

Etsy (ETSY) is scheduled to report its Q4 results before the market opens on Wednesday. Both Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system and Wall Street analysts recommend holding the stock, amid concerns over valuation.

SA Investing Group Leader Michael Wiggins De Oliveira takes an even more aggressively bearish view, suggesting investors sell the stock. The analyst pointed out that Etsy's $1.2 billion net debt limits its ability to generate meaningful shareholder value. He added that share repurchases are unlikely to support the stock price without stronger growth fundamentals.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $1.46
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $861.46M
  • Earnings Insight: ETSY has topped EPS estimates in 5 of the past 8 quarters, missing revenue expectations only once in that time span.

Also reporting: Vale S.A. (VALE), Rio Tinto Group (RIO), Fiverr International (FVRR), Bausch Health Companies (BHC), Innovative Industrial Properties (IIPR), Tanger (SKT), SolarEdge Technologies (SEDG), Analog Devices (ADI), Exact Sciences (EXAS), Appian (APPN), HSBC Holdings (HSBC), Ionis Pharmaceuticals (IONS), New York Mortgage Trust (NYMT), Wix.com (WIX), BioMarin Pharmaceutical (BMRN), Inseego (INSG), CF Industries Holdings (CF), American Water Works Company (AWK), Carvana (CVNA), Blue Owl Capital Corporation (OBDC) and more.

Thursday, February 20

Alibaba (BABA)

Alibaba (BABA) is set to release its quarterly financial results before the market opens on Thursday.

Amid growing excitement around DeepSeek, Alibaba's domestic AI rival, Alibaba Cloud, recently introduced the Qwen2.5-VL family of artificial intelligence models. Launched in the last week of January 2025, these models are designed to perform various text and image analysis tasks.

SA Investing Group leader Envision Research, who has a Buy rating on the stock, observed that BABA is making another attempt to break the $120 level. He cites both technical and fundamental reasons for believing this attempt will succeed, potentially unlocking further upside.

While Wall Street analysts continue to rate the stock as a Buy, with shares having surged by as much as 67% over the past year, Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system takes a more cautious stance, assigning the stock a Hold due to concerns over its valuation and growth potential.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $2.70
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $38.25B
  • Earnings Insight: The company has beaten EPS estimates in 6 of the past 8 quarters, and revenue estimates in 3 of those reports.

Also reporting: Walmart (WMT), Newmont (NEM), Unity Software (U), MercadoLibre (MELI), The Southern Company (SO), Booking Holdings (BKNG), Rivian Automotive (RIVN), VICI Properties (VICI), Dropbox (DBX), Cheniere Energy (LNG), Consolidated Edison (ED), Jumia Technologies AG (JMIA), MP Materials (MP), Intellia Therapeutics (NTLA), Frontier Communications Parent (FYBR), Wayfair (W), Dynavax Technologies (DVAX), Universal Display (OLED), Akamai Technologies (AKAM), Bilibili (BILI) and more.

Friday, February 21

The end of the week will see relatively few earnings reports with Uniti Group (UNIT), MFA Financial (MFA), Vipshop Holdings (VIPS), Five9 (FIVN), AdvanSix (ASIX), all scheduled to report their earnings in the pre-market hours.

data.title: Duke Energy yanks climate language from earnings report with new focus on natural gas
data.description:
As the Trump administration shifts U.S. energy policy away from clean alternatives, some power companies are following suit by removing the mention of climate targets in their public reports.

Duke Energy's (NYSE:DUK) Q4 earnings release looked similar to its Q3 earnings release, but the company removed a mention of climate targets in the latest release, as it builds more natural gas plants and reconsiders its coal plans.

In its Q3 release last year, Duke (NYSE:DUK) said it was "executing an ambitious clean energy transition, keeping reliability, affordability and accessibility at the forefront," and noted its goals to emit net zero methane by 2030 and net zero carbon by 2050.

Duke's (DUK) Q4 release this week said it was “executing an ambitious energy transition," removing terms such as "clean," "affordability" and "accessibility" as well as any mention of lowering emissions.

"The strategy hasn’t changed,"CEO Lynn Good told Bloomberg, but "we do see an uptick in gas generation that wouldn't have been the case five years ago just as we put together plans on how we are going to serve the load in this moment."

"While our long-term business strategy and our climate goals remain the same, we recently adjusted the language in our company boilerplate to emphasize the near-term, essential nature of meeting our customers' evolving needs and the increasing growth in front of us," a Duke (DUK) representative said.

Duke (DUK) is launching construction on more than 2 GW of natural gas generation in North Carolina and South Carolina, and expects to have ~5 GW of new natural gas in service by the end of 2029, and the company also is building out clean power sources like solar farms with plans to add 10 GW of renewable energy by 2029.

data.title: Amazon warehouse workers in North Carolina vote against joining union
data.description:
Workers at an Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) warehouse voted against union representation at a warehouse in North Carolina, the company said Saturday.

According to results posted on the website of the union, Carolina Amazons United for Solidarity and Empowerment, or CAUSE, 75% of eligible workers at the Garner warehouse near Raleigh voted against representation while 25% voted in favor. Some 3,276 voted, and the warehouse reportedly employs 4,300 workers.

Amazon says hourly pay starts at $18.50, and the union campaigned on $30 per hour and longer breaks.

“We're glad that our team in Garner was able to have their voices heard, and that they chose to keep a direct relationship with Amazon,” an Amazon spokesperson told Seeking Alpha in an email. “We look forward to continuing to make this a great place to work together, and to supporting our teammates as they build their futures with us."

Earlier in the day, Reuters reported that CAUSE accused Amazon of interfering with voting on Saturday. It reportedly said it wasn’t able to display a livestream of votes online because the company made three workers who were to report the results leave.

The Amazon spokesperson denied the accusation.

Neither CAUSE nor the National Labor Relations Board immediately responded to a request for comment by Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Intel powers to best weekly advance in over 25 years
data.description:
Shares of Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) rocketed 23.6% over the past five trading days, marking the stock's best weekly performance since mid-January 2000. Much of the gain was on speculation about some sort of tie up between the legacy firm and the world's largest contract chipmaker, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM).

The troubled 56-year-old chipmaker has fallen behind rivals in the artificial intelligence (AI) race and has struggled with sales.

Last year, the firm booted Pat Gelsinger as top boss, suspended quarterly dividends and announced layoffs as part of a $10B cost-reduction plan. Still, the stock cratered about 60% in 2024. Intel's (NASDAQ:INTC) quarterly report last month showed the company swinging to a full-year loss on an annual revenue decline of 2%. It also offered up soft guidance for Q1 2025.

Intel's (NASDAQ:INTC) surge this week was driven by several factors including favorable remarks on AI by the U.S. government, the speculation regarding Taiwan Semi (TSM), and a positive reaction to its latest processor - with some reviewers identifying it as the fastest on the market.

On Tuesday, Vice President JD Vance at a summit in Paris said that the most advanced AI chips will be made in the U.S.

A day later, Baird analyst Tristan Gerra said the brokerage had heard discussions from the supply chain in Asia about a potential spin-off of Intel's (NASDAQ:INTC) foundry unit, possibly involving Taiwan Semi (TSM).

Bloomberg News on Friday reported Taiwan Semi (TSM) was considering taking a controlling stake in Intel’s (NASDAQ:INTC) factories at the request of Trump administration officials, citing a person familiar with the matter.

Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) interim co-CEOs David Zinsner and Michelle Johnston Holthaus on last month's earnings conference call acknowledged that there was still work to be done.

"From day one, we have been working closely together alongside the board to drive better execution of our strategy. There are no quick fixes," Holthaus had said.

data.title: Reddit plans to introduce paywalls this year
data.description:
Reddit (NYSE:RDDT) chief executive Steve Huffman said on Thursday the company is planning to launch a paywall this year, marking a major shift for the online forum founded on the premise of widely and freely sharing information.

"It’s a work in progress right now, so that one’s coming," said Huffman in response to questions about the company's plans to create content that only paid members can view.

Huffman said during a Reddit (RDDT) "Ask Me Anything" session that the message board site was planning to roll out "paid subreddits" in 2025.

The company's paywall would only apply to new subreddits and not on any of the subreddits currently available.

data.title: What’s ahead for Eli Lilly and Novo as RFK Jr. takes over HHS
data.description:
Leading obesity drug makers Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) and Novo Nordisk (NVO) have found themselves in two contrasting positions as Robert F. Kennedy Jr., a leading critic of weight loss drugs, takes the helm of the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS).

Since his nomination in November until the swearing-in ceremony this week, Novo Nordisk (NVO) has lost more than 23%, while Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) has gained ~13%, outperforming the S&P 500’s (SP500) ~4% gain.

RFK. Jr. openly criticized America’s reliance on weight loss drugs during his brief presidential run. After dropping out and endorsing Trump, he launched the "Make America Healthy Again" initiative, making it a key theme of his so-called MAHA movement.

"They're counting on selling [weight-loss medications] to Americans because we're so stupid and so addicted to drugs,” the 71-year-old vaccine critic said during an interview with Fox News in October. "If we just gave good food, three meals a day, to every man, woman, and child in our country, we could solve the obesity and diabetes epidemic overnight.”

With healthcare professionals set to take over the FDA and Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) under Trump, it is unlikely that RFK. Jr.’s views alone will prevail in regulatory decision-making.

Dr. Mehmet Oz, Trump’s pick for CMS leadership, has spent years promoting weight loss drugs as a TV host, while Marty Makary, Trump’s nominee to lead the FDA, is the chief medical officer of Sesame, a telehealth firm that offers compounded weight loss drugs among other services.

Despite concerns related to RFK Jr., Eli Lilly’s (NYSE:LLY) gain indicates that Novo’s (NVO) selloff has more to do with its own challenges.

In December, the company lost more than $70B in market cap in a single day after announcing that its next-gen obesity candidate, CagriSema, caused only ~20% of weight loss in a Phase 3 trial, missing the 25% mark the Danish drugmaker set in the lead-up to the readout.

The company is also battling compounded weight loss drugmakers such as Hims & Hers Health (HIMS), which offer cheaper versions of its GLP-1 product semaglutide, capitalizing on a regulatory loophole amid a nationwide shortage.

Seeking Alpha analyst Stephen Ayers reaffirmed his Buy rating on Novo (NVO) after RFK Jr.’s nomination, arguing that recent concerns over his appointment “and supply constraint issues are likely temporary and overblown.”

“We can reasonably hope that science and the checks and balances inherent in the U.S. healthcare system will prevail,” the analyst wrote, adding, “While RFK Jr. will undoubtedly influence drug development and marketing, major reform will remain difficult to come by.”
